skill.name: python-observability-patterns
skill.description: Guides Python teams in adding structured logging, Prometheus metrics, and OpenTelemetry tracing to production services.
skill.summary: Add production observability patterns to Python services with logging, metrics, and tracing guidance.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the command-execution, env-access, script, reconnaissance, and weak-crypto alerts were false positives in Markdown examples or reference tables. The only retained risk factor is network-related sample code in tracing documentation, including a local OTLP endpoint and a placeholder external service call.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The cited lines are Python fenced code examples in documentation, and no shell execution or subprocess usage was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown Python code fences and demonstrates observability libraries. No evidence of runtime shell command execution was found.

low_findings.title: Credential Access Alert Is a Redaction Key List
low_findings.description: The env_access alert is a false positive. The line defines sensitive field names for log redaction and does not read environment variables or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses a static set containing names such as password, token, secret, api_key, and authorization. It replaces matching log fields with a redacted marker.

low_findings.title: Network Examples Require Deployment Review
low_findings.description: The tracing reference includes a local OTLP exporter with insecure transport and a placeholder HTTP POST to api.example.com. These are documentation examples, not skill-executed network calls, but users should configure production endpoints and transport security carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are visible and semantically legitimate for tracing examples. Risk is low because they are sample snippets rather than autonomous skill behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Dynamic Import Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and dynamic import alerts do not correspond to cryptographic operations or dynamic module loading. The cited tracing sampling section imports named OpenTelemetry sampler classes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context shows normal import syntax and documentation metadata. No md5, sha1, eval-style import, or cryptographic API usage was found.

content.value_statement: Python services need consistent logs, metrics, and traces to diagnose production issues. This skill gives Codex, Claude, and Claude Code practical patterns for structlog, Prometheus, and OpenTelemetry.
actual_capabilities: Shows structlog setup for JSON logs and context variables.
Provides FastAPI middleware examples for request IDs and metrics.
Explains Prometheus counters, histograms, gauges, summaries, and collectors.
Demonstrates OpenTelemetry tracer setup, spans, context propagation, and sampling.
Includes a reusable Python logging configuration asset.
limitations: Does not install dependencies or configure infrastructure automatically.
Examples assume Python 3.10 and common FastAPI service patterns.
Exporter endpoints and metric labels must be adapted for each environment.
Does not provide vendor-specific dashboards or alert rules.

use_cases.title: Instrument a Python API
use_cases.description: Add request IDs, JSON logs, and Prometheus metrics to a FastAPI service.

use_cases.title: Standardize Service Telemetry
use_cases.description: Create consistent logging, metric naming, and tracing conventions across Python services.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Debugging
use_cases.description: Connect logs and traces so incidents can be investigated faster.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Structured Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add structured JSON logging to my Python service. Include request IDs and safe log fields.

prompt_templates.title: Add FastAPI Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add Prometheus metrics to my FastAPI app. Track request count, latency, status, and active requests.

prompt_templates.title: Design Tracing for a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to instrument my order workflow with OpenTelemetry spans, errors, attributes, and context propagation.

prompt_templates.title: Review Observability Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Python service observability. Identify missing logs, metrics, traces, cardinality risks, and sensitive data risks.

output_examples.input: Add request logging to a FastAPI endpoint.
output_examples.output: A concise middleware plan with request ID propagation, structured event names, status codes, timing, and cleanup steps.

output_examples.input: Create metrics for a checkout service.
output_examples.output: A metrics design covering counters for orders, histograms for latency and value, gauges for active work, and low-cardinality labels.
A tracing outline with parent and child spans, error recording, safe attributes, sampling guidance, and log correlation fields.

best_practices: Keep metric labels low-cardinality and avoid user IDs or request IDs as labels.
Redact passwords, tokens, authorization headers, and personal data before logs are emitted.
Use consistent service names, trace IDs, span IDs, and request IDs across services.
anti_patterns: Logging raw request bodies, tokens, or customer data without redaction.
Creating metrics with unbounded labels such as user ID or request ID.
Using insecure telemetry exporters in production without transport controls.

faq.question: Does this skill run monitoring agents?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples for adding observability code to Python applications.

faq.question: Which Python libraries does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers structlog, prometheus-client, and OpenTelemetry examples for common service patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with FastAPI services?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include FastAPI middleware for request context, metrics, and tracing.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance for logs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes redaction patterns for sensitive fields and email addresses.

faq.question: Can it generate dashboards?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on application instrumentation, not dashboard or alert configuration.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production systems?
faq.answer: Yes, with review of endpoints, sampling, labels, and sensitive data handling for your environment.
